tasks.title: Backfill missing request and timestamps after scan or task build
evidence: scan_cmd and task_cmd do not fill request or created_at and updated_at if runner omits them
plan: Detect newly added tasks lacking request or timestamps
Populate missing fields using the input request and current UTC time
Add tests for backfill behavior
notes: Added backfill_missing_fields function to queue.rs
Updated scan_cmd.rs to backfill new tasks after runner succeeds
Updated task_cmd.rs to backfill new tasks after runner succeeds
Added 6 tests covering backfill behavior (populate, skip existing, empty string handling)
All tests pass and make ci completes successfully

tasks.title: Validate RFC3339 timestamps in set_status and queue validation
evidence: set_status only checks now is non-empty and validation never parses timestamps
plan: Parse timestamps with time::OffsetDateTime and reject invalid formats
Validate created_at updated_at and completed_at when present
Add tests for invalid timestamp handling
notes: Added RFC3339 validation for created_at, updated_at, and completed_at in queue.rs
Updated set_status to validate incoming timestamps
Added parsing and macros features to time crate dependency
Added regression tests for invalid timestamp formats

tasks.title: Introduce canonical .env and .env.example and load dotenv in CLI
evidence: project policy requires a canonical .env file but the repo has none
plan: Add .env and .env.example with non-secret defaults
Load dotenv early in CLI startup
Document expected variables and usage
notes: Added .env and .env.example with RALPH_STRESS_BURN_IN=0
Added dotenvy = "0.15" to crates/ralph/Cargo.toml
Loaded dotenv early in crates/ralph/src/main.rs using dotenvy::dotenv().ok()
Verified with make ci

tasks.title: Handle stale queue locks via auto-clean or force unlock command
evidence: acquire_dir_lock only reports stale pid and requires manual removal of .ralph/lock
plan: Add stale lock detection and an opt-in auto-clean or queue unlock command
Ensure lock cleanup is safe when the pid is still active
Add tests for stale lock cleanup behavior
notes: Added `force` flag to `acquire_dir_lock` and `acquire_queue_lock` to auto-clean stale locks.
Added global `--force` flag to CLI to opt-in to stale lock cleanup.
Added `ralph queue unlock` command to manually remove the lock directory.
Added regression tests in `crates/ralph/tests/stale_lock_cleanup_test.rs`.
Ensured lock cleanup is skipped if the PID is still active, even with `--force`.

tasks.title: Refactor main.rs to centralize queue loading and validation
evidence: handle_queue repeats loading/validation boilerplate in 5+ subcommands
plan: Extract common load/validate logic into a helper function in main.rs or a new module
Update handle_queue subcommands to use the helper
Verify no regression in error messages or behavior
notes: Extracted load_and_validate_queues helper function in crates/ralph/src/main.rs.
Updated handle_queue to use the helper in Validate, Next, NextId, Show, and List commands.
Verified functionality with cargo run -p ralph -- queue validate and make ci.

tasks.title: Centralize RunnerKind and Model resolution logic
evidence: resolve_agent_args (main.rs) and resolve_run_agent_settings (run_cmd.rs) overlap significantly
plan: Consolidate resolution logic into runner.rs or a dedicated settings module
Ensure consistent precedence: CLI > task.agent > project config > global config > built-in
Update main.rs, task_cmd.rs, scan_cmd.rs, and run_cmd.rs to use the centralized logic
notes: Created `runner::AgentSettings` struct and `resolve_agent_settings` function in `crates/ralph/src/runner.rs`.
Updated `crates/ralph/src/main.rs` to use `resolve_agent_settings` for `task` and `scan` commands.
Updated `crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs` to use `resolve_agent_settings` for `run one` and `run loop`.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Audit and harden YAML repair logic in queue.rs
evidence: repair_yaml_scalars uses brittle string manipulation and basic prefix/substring checks
plan: Expand test suite with complex/nested YAML breakage
Explore more robust repair strategies (e.g., partial parsing or selective escaping)
Ensure repair logic handles all standard scalar types and edge-case colons
notes: Added comprehensive test suite in `crates/ralph/tests/yaml_repair_test.rs` covering edge cases.
Hardened `repair_yaml_scalars` to prevent quoting of task object fields (e.g. `id`, `title`) when indented > 2 spaces.
Introduced `is_task_field_key` and `looks_like_task_start` helper functions.
Verified fixes with new tests and existing stress tests.

tasks.title: Enhance doctor command to validate done.yaml and check Makefile
evidence: doctor currently ignores the done archive and doesn't check for CI readiness (Makefile)
plan: Add validation for done.yaml in run_doctor
Check for presence of Makefile and 'ci' target
Add check for common config errors (e.g., runner/model mismatches)
notes: Added validation for done.yaml in doctor command.
Added check for Makefile presence and ci target in doctor.
Added model/runner compatibility check in doctor.
Updated doctor_contract_test.rs to include Makefile in test environments and added a new test for done.yaml failure.

tasks.title: Audit TaskStatus serialization for consistent snake_case
evidence: internal enums are mapped manually; ensure serde(rename_all = "snake_case") is respected everywhere
plan: Verify all TaskStatus usages in YAML match "todo", "doing", "done"
Audit other enums (Runner, Model, ReasoningEffort) for consistency
notes: Audited crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs and confirmed all public enums (ProjectType, Runner, ReasoningEffort, TaskStatus) use
Audited crates/ralph/src/main.rs and confirmed all ValueEnum implementations (StatusArg, QueueShowFormat, QueueListFormat) use
Verified Model uses manual serialization with specific kebab-case strings which is consistent with its cross-tool requirements.
Verified all current TaskStatus usages in .ralph/queue.yaml and .ralph/done.yaml correctly use "todo", "doing", and "done".
Confirmed is_task_field_key in queue.rs uses consistent snake_case for field matching.
make ci passed successfully.

tasks.title: Improve gitutil::require_clean_repo error message
evidence: current error bails with raw porcelain output; lacks actionable bypass/stash advice
plan: Update error message to suggest 'git stash' or using '--force' (if applicable)
Distinguish between untracked and tracked changes in the message
notes: Updated `require_clean_repo` to parse `git status --porcelain` and distinguish tracked vs untracked changes.
Added helpful suggestions (`git stash`, `git commit`, `git clean -fd`, `git add`) and mentioned `--force`.
Added a `force` parameter to `require_clean_repo` to allow bypassing the check via the global `--force` CLI flag.
Updated all callers in `run_cmd.rs`, `scan_cmd.rs`, and `task_cmd.rs` to pass the `force` flag.
Added integration test `run_one_succeeds_when_repo_is_dirty_and_force_is_used` in `git_repo_integration_test.rs`.

tasks.title: Expand redaction.rs with more sensitive patterns
evidence: current redaction is limited to bearer tokens and some env var keys
plan: Add patterns for AWS keys, SSH private key headers, and generic hex tokens
Improve key-value pair detection for multi-line values
Add tests for new patterns
notes: Added AWS key redaction (AKIA and 40-char secrets).
Added SSH private key redaction (BEGIN/END markers).
Added generic hex token redaction (32+ chars).
Improved key-value pair redaction to handle YAML-style multi-line indicators (| and >).
Added regression tests in `crates/ralph/tests/redaction_expansion_test.rs`.

tasks.title: Integrate a logging crate (e.g., env_logger) for output control
evidence: direct println/eprintln usage makes verbosity control difficult
plan: Add env_logger or tracing-subscriber dependency
Replace strategic println/eprintln with log macros (info!, warn!, debug!)
Allow controlling verbosity via RUST_LOG or a --verbose flag
notes: Added `log` and `env_logger` dependencies.
Added global `--verbose` flag to CLI (sets log level to `debug`).
Initialized `env_logger` with `info` as default level.
Replaced strategic `println!`/`eprintln!` calls with `log` macros across `main.rs`, `queue.rs`, `run_cmd.rs`, `scan_cmd.rs`, and `task_cmd.rs`.
Updated `git_repo_integration_test.rs` to handle new log format.

tasks.title: Improve run_loop reporting and remaining tasks count
evidence: run_loop doesn't report how many todo tasks remain or total progress
plan: Count initial todo tasks before starting loop
Report "Task X of Y" or "Z tasks remaining" in the loop output
notes: Count initial todo tasks at the start of the loop.
Log "Starting run loop with X todo tasks."
Log "Completed task_id (X/Y)" after each successful task.
Log "No more todo tasks remaining." when the queue is exhausted.

tasks.title: Enhance handle_init to verify existing file validity
evidence: init just prints "exists" for files; it doesn't check if they are valid or need repair
plan: Add validation/repair logic to handle_init for existing queue/done/config files
Report "exists (valid)" or "exists (repaired)" instead of just "exists"
notes: Added validation and repair logic to `init` command for existing queue, done, and config files.
Updated `InitReport` and `FileInitStatus` to track whether files were created, valid, or repaired.
Updated `main.rs` to report "exists (valid)" or "exists (repaired)" in the output.
Added unit test `init_repairs_existing_when_not_forced` to verify repair behavior.
Verified all tests pass with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Improve runner::run_prompt error reporting with structured types
evidence: runner errors are currently generic anyhow::Error, making recovery decisions harder
plan: Define a RunnerError enum (BinaryMissing, Timeout, NonZeroExit, etc.)
Update run_prompt and its callers to use structured errors
Improve supervisor response to specific failure modes (e.g., don't revert if it's just a timeout)
notes: Defined `RunnerError` enum with `BinaryMissing`, `SpawnFailed`, `NonZeroExit`, `TerminatedBySignal`, `Interrupted`, `Timeout`, and `Io` variants.
Updated `runner::run_prompt` and its sub-functions to return `Result<RunnerOutput, RunnerError>`.
Added `timeout` support to `run_prompt` and `wait_for_child`.
Updated `run_cmd.rs`, `task_cmd.rs`, and `scan_cmd.rs` to handle structured errors and log specific details.
Implemented "no revert on timeout" logic in `run_cmd.rs`.
Added `thiserror` dependency to `crates/ralph/Cargo.toml`.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Audit config.rs for robust error handling and layering
evidence: apply_layer uses manual field-by-field copy; missing some validation checks
plan: Audit apply_layer for missing fields or inconsistent merging
Ensure all Option fields in ConfigLayer are correctly handled
Add more descriptive errors for invalid config values
notes: Refactored `apply_layer` to use centralized `merge_from` methods on `QueueConfig` and `AgentConfig`.
Added `validate_config` function to ensure all configuration values (version, id_prefix, id_width, file paths, and runner binaries) are valid and non-empty.
Improved error context by wrapping `apply_layer` calls with `with_context` in `resolve_from_cwd`.
Removed redundant validation from `resolve_id_prefix` and others while maintaining safety.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Add stress tests for edge-case YAML breakage and recovery
evidence: current stress tests focus on volume, not necessarily complex structural breakage
plan: Add specific test cases for truncated YAML, invalid types, and nested scalar colons
Verify that Ralph reliably repairs or bails with clear errors
notes: Added `stress_queue_repair_edge_cases` test covering truncated YAML, invalid types, and nested scalar colons.
Improved `repair_yaml_structure` to proactively fix version mismatch and tasks indentation.
Refined `repair_yaml_scalars` to correctly distinguish between task starts and list items needing quotes.
Fixed Clippy warnings in `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs`.

tasks.title: Audit CLI help strings for completeness and consistency
evidence: some subcommands have more detailed help/examples than others
plan: Review all long_help and after_long_help strings in main.rs
Ensure consistent formatting for examples and runner/model notes
Verify all subcommands have at least one clear example
notes: Added about and after_long_help with examples to all subcommands in main.rs.
Added doc comments to StatusArg, QueueShowFormat, and QueueListFormat for better ValueEnum help.
Ensured consistent example formatting across all commands.
Fixed missing about for scan and init subcommands.
Verified with cargo run -p ralph -- --help and subcommands.

tasks.title: Update all rust cargo dependencies to the latest version and run `make ci` to verify everything still works
evidence: some dependencies are out of date
plan: Update all dependencies to the latest version
Run make ci to verify everything still works
notes: Updated `thiserror` from 1.0 to 2.0 in `crates/ralph/Cargo.toml`.
Updated all other dependencies to their exact latest versions in `crates/ralph/Cargo.toml`.
Verified with `make ci` (all 153 tests passed).
Confirmed `Cargo.lock` is updated and synchronized.

tasks.title: Add .ralph/README.md creation to `ralph init` when loop prompts reference it
evidence: loop prompts can include .ralph/README.md, but init does not ensure it exists
plan: Detect when loop prompts reference .ralph/README.md
Create .ralph/README.md during `ralph init` when referenced
Add tests to cover prompt references and init behavior
notes: Added `prompts_reference_readme` to `prompts.rs` to detect references in active prompts.
Added `crates/ralph/assets/ralph_readme.md` as default content for `.ralph/README.md`.
Updated `ralph init` to create `.ralph/README.md` if referenced and missing (or if forced).
Updated `main.rs` to report `readme` initialization status.
Added unit tests in `init_cmd.rs` covering both referenced and unreferenced scenarios.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Standardize top-level error formatting and redaction in main.rs
evidence:
Top-level error handling in `main.rs` uses `eprintln!("Error: {:#}", err);`.
This is unformatted and does not redact potential secrets contained in error messages.
plan: Integrate `redaction::redact_text` into the top-level error printing in `main.rs`.
Use consistent formatting (e.g., bold or color) for "Error:" prefix.
Ensure nested errors (via `{:#}`) are also redacted.
notes: Integrated redaction::redact_text into top-level error handling in main.rs.
Standardized "Error:" prefix with bold red formatting.
Verified with cargo run and manual error triggering.

tasks.title: Refactor doctor_cmd.rs to use standardized logging and levels
evidence:
`doctor_cmd.rs` uses `println!` for almost everything, which ignores the `log` crate
and user verbosity settings. It also manually manages "OK/FAIL" prefixes.
plan: Replace `println!` calls with appropriate `log` levels (info, warn, error).
Use a helper for consistent "OK/FAIL/WARN" status reporting.
Ensure the output respects the `--verbose` flag via the logger configuration.
notes: Replaced all `println!` and `eprintln!` calls in `doctor_cmd.rs` with `log` macros.
Introduced `log_success`, `log_warn`, and `log_error` in `outpututil.rs` for consistent status reporting.
Updated `doctor_contract_test.rs` to verify new log output format and destination (stderr).
Verified that `make ci` passes with 159 tests.

tasks.title: Implement centralized CLI output utility for consistent formatting
evidence:
`outpututil.rs` is currently very minimal. CLI output patterns like task lists,
status reports, and error summaries are implemented ad-hoc in various command modules.
plan: Expand `outpututil.rs` with helpers for common CLI output patterns (e.g., info/success/warning prefixes).
Add support for basic ANSI colors/styles if appropriate (mimicking project conventions).
Provide a standardized way to print task summaries used by `queue list` and `run`.
notes: Added `colored` dependency to `crates/ralph/Cargo.toml`.
Expanded `outpututil.rs` with `print_info`, `print_success`, `print_warn`, `print_error` helpers.
Implemented `format_task_compact` and `format_task_detailed` in `outpututil.rs` with status coloring.
Refactored `main.rs` to use the centralized formatting logic.

tasks.title: Standardize anyhow::bail! messages to include fix direction
evidence:
Many `bail!` messages in `config.rs` and `queue.rs` simply state what is wrong
without providing guidance on how the user can fix it (e.g., "id_prefix must be non-empty").
plan: Audit `bail!` calls in core logic.
Update error messages to follow a "Problem: Context. Solution: Action." pattern.
Example: "id_prefix is empty. Set a valid prefix in .ralph/config.yaml or via --id-prefix."
notes: Updated 30+ `bail!` calls in `config.rs`, `queue.rs`, `run_cmd.rs`, `task_cmd.rs`, `scan_cmd.rs`, `prompts.rs`, `main.rs`, and `doctor_cmd.rs` to follow the "Problem: Solution" pattern.
Ensured that error messages provide actionable advice (e.g., specific config keys or flags to set).
Updated unit tests and contract tests to reflect the new improved error messages.
Verified that `make ci` passes with all tests updated.

tasks.title: Implement a logging wrapper with automatic redaction
evidence:
Redaction is currently applied manually in some places (like `run_cmd.rs` error handling)
but forgotten in others. A centralized logging wrapper would ensure consistency.
plan: Implement a macro or helper that wraps standard `log` macros to apply `redact_text`.
Alternatively, implement a custom `Log` implementation that redacts all messages.
Ensure that all sensitive data (env vars, keys) is automatically masked in all logs.
notes: Implemented `RedactedLogger` as a `log::Log` wrapper that automatically redacts all log messages using `redact_text`.
Added `rinfo!`, `rwarn!`, `rerror!`, `rdebug!`, and `rtrace!` macros for explicit redacted logging.
Integrated `RedactedLogger` into `main.rs` to wrap the global logger.
Added top-of-file purpose header to `redaction.rs` as per AGENTS.md.

tasks.title: Improve fsutil::acquire_dir_lock error messages
evidence:
`acquire_dir_lock` builds a very long, complex error message as a single string.
It's hard to read and doesn't clearly separate the state from the suggested fix.
plan: Refactor the error message generation in `acquire_dir_lock` for better readability.
Use newlines and clear sections (Lock Holder, Started At, Suggested Action).
Ensure the message provides specific `rm` commands for the lock directory.
notes: Refactored `acquire_dir_lock` to use a structured error message format.
Added `format_lock_error` helper for clear separation of state and suggested actions.
Updated `stale_lock_cleanup_test.rs` to reflect the new message format.
Verified that `make ci` passes with the changes.

tasks.title: Refactor gitutil.rs to use thiserror for structured errors
evidence:
`gitutil.rs` uses a manual `GitPushError` struct and `classify_push_error` that
just returns strings for `bail!`. It doesn't leverage `thiserror` for real types.
plan: Define a proper `GitError` enum using `thiserror`.
Include variants for common failures: NoUpstream, AuthFailed, DirtyRepo, etc.
Update call sites to use these structured errors for better programmatic handling and consistent display.
notes: Defined `GitError` enum using `thiserror` in `gitutil.rs`.
Replaced `GitPushError` with `GitError` variants (NoUpstream, AuthFailed, PushFailed, DirtyRepo, CommandFailed).
Updated `gitutil` functions to return `Result<T, GitError>`.
Refactored `run_cmd::push_if_ahead` to use structured error matching for skipping push when no upstream is configured.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Add debug logging for config resolution and repo root discovery
evidence:
`find_repo_root` and `resolve_from_cwd` operate silently. If configuration
loading fails or picks the wrong directory, it's difficult to debug without code changes.
plan: Add `log::debug!` statements to `find_repo_root` showing each directory checked.
Log the paths of global and project config files being loaded.
Log the final resolved paths for queue, done, and root.
notes: Added `log::debug!` statements to `find_repo_root` showing each directory checked during root discovery.
Added debug logs for global and project config loading in `resolve_from_cwd`.
Logged final resolved paths for `repo_root`, `queue_path`, and `done_path`.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Integrate redaction into RunnerOutput and RunnerError display
evidence:
`RunnerError::NonZeroExit` and `TerminatedBySignal` store raw stdout/stderr strings.
While `run_cmd.rs` redacts them before logging, other consumers might not.
plan: Ensure that `RunnerError` formatting (via `thiserror`) applies redaction to stdout/stderr.
Audit all places where `RunnerOutput` is displayed to ensure redaction is applied.
notes: Implemented `RedactedString` wrapper in `redaction.rs` that automatically redacts on `Display` and `Debug`.
Updated `RunnerError` to use `RedactedString` for `stdout` and `stderr` fields, ensuring automatic redaction via `thiserror`.
Implemented `Display` and `Debug` for `RunnerOutput` to ensure its content is always redacted when displayed.
Simplified call sites in `scan_cmd.rs`, `task_cmd.rs`, and `run_cmd.rs` by leveraging automatic redaction.
Added unit tests in `runner.rs` to verify redaction in `RunnerError` and `RunnerOutput`.

tasks.title: Add log statements for task execution lifecycle and duration
evidence:
Task execution currently logs completion but doesn't log the start or the
duration of the run. This makes it hard to track progress in long-running loops.
plan: Add a log message when a task starts execution, including its ID and title.
Record the start time and log the total duration on completion.
Log the runner and model being used for the specific task.
notes: Added a log message when a task starts execution, including its ID, title, runner, and model.
Recorded the start time using `std::time::Instant`.
Logged the total duration on successful completion.
Updated `crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs` to implement these lifecycle logs.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Standardize log vs println! usage across command handlers
evidence:
Command handlers in `main.rs` (like `handle_queue`, `handle_config`) mix `log::info!`
and `println!`. Status messages should ideally use the logger to allow filtering.
plan: Audit `handle_*` functions in `main.rs`.
Move status/info messages to `log::info!`.
Keep `println!` only for primary command output (e.g., task IDs, YAML output).
notes: Audited `handle_*` functions in `main.rs` for `println!` vs `log::info!` usage.
Refactored `handle_init` to use `log::info!` for status reporting.
Confirmed `handle_queue` status messages already use `log::info!`.
Maintained `println!` for primary command output (IDs, YAML, Config Paths).
Verified that `make ci` passes with all tests reflecting new log output format.

tasks.title: Enhance queue validation errors with fix instructions
evidence:
`validate_task_required_fields` and `validate_task_id` provide exact errors but
could be more helpful (e.g., "id must contain '-'" could suggest the correct format).
plan: Update validation error messages to include examples of valid values.
Suggest specific fixes (e.g., "run 'ralph queue repair' if the YAML is slightly malformed").
notes: Updated `validate_task_required_fields`, `validate_task_id`, `ensure_list_non_empty`, `ensure_field_present`, and `validate_rfc3339` in `queue.rs`.
Error messages now include examples of valid values (IDs, timestamps) and suggest running 'ralph queue repair' for malformed YAML.
Standardized "Problem: Solution" pattern for all validation bailouts.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Implement RedactedError wrapper for safe error display
evidence:
There is no easy way to wrap an arbitrary `anyhow::Error` and ensure it is
redacted when displayed. This leads to manual redaction logic at various display sites.
plan: Create a `RedactedError(anyhow::Error)` wrapper in `redaction.rs`.
Implement `Display` and `Debug` for it, applying `redact_text` to the output.
Provide a helper method `redacted()` for `Result` to easily wrap errors.
notes: Implemented `RedactedError` struct wrapping `anyhow::Error`.
Implemented `Display` and `Debug` for `RedactedError` to automatically apply redaction.
Added `Redactable` trait with `redacted()` helper for `Result`.
Added unit tests for `RedactedError` and `Redactable`.

tasks.title: Audit and improve logging in fsutil sync operations
evidence:
`sync_dir_best_effort` and other fs operations fail silently or without much
context. While "best effort" implies silence, debug logs would be valuable.
plan: Add `log::debug!` or `log::trace!` to file synchronization and atomic write steps.
Include path information in these logs to help trace filesystem issues.
notes: Added debug logging to sync_dir_best_effort, write_atomic, acquire_dir_lock, and write_lock_owner.

tasks.title: Refactor task formatting to use centralized utility
evidence:
`format_task_compact` and `format_task_long` are defined in `main.rs`.
They should be moved to `outpututil.rs` or `contracts.rs` and standardized.
plan: Move task formatting logic out of `main.rs`.
Ensure consistent trimming and joining of tags/scope.
Integrate with the new `outpututil` helpers for consistent CLI presentation.
notes: Standardized `format_task_compact` and `format_task_detailed` in `outpututil.rs` with consistent `\t` separators.
Extracted `join_csv_trimmed` as a public helper in `outpututil.rs`.
Added `format_task_id` and `format_task_id_title` to `outpututil.rs` and used them in `main.rs`.
Centralized `format_task_commit_message` in `outpututil.rs` and used it in `run_cmd.rs`.
Verified with `make ci`.

tasks.title: Audit and remove dead or duplicated code with shared helpers
scope: repo-wide scan for duplication and dead code
evidence: User request: Thorough review of dead code and duplicated code/redunandant code. Make sure we are not violating DRY principals and all code is consistenly using helpers, etc.
plan: Scan the repo for dead code, unused modules, and duplicated logic; inventory candidates with file paths.
Identify shared patterns that should be centralized and propose/implement helpers to remove duplication.
Remove dead code and refactor duplicates to use shared helpers, keeping behavior unchanged and tests updated.
Run relevant checks/tests and ensure consistency with repo standards.
notes: Centralized runner error handling and log tailing in `crates/ralph/src/runutil.rs`.
Reused shared helpers for runner binaries, task diffing, and timestamp fallback across scan/task/run.
Removed unused `gitutil::require_clean_repo` and `outpututil::log_info`.
Verified with `make ci`.
tasks.request: Thorough review of dead code and duplicated code/redunandant code. Make sure we are not violating DRY principals and all code is consistenly using helpers, etc.

tasks.title: Prevent redaction panic on non-UTF-8 boundary slicing
evidence: User request: Error: Provider returned error thread 'main' (15281963) panicked at crates/ralph/src/redaction.rs:235:16: byte index 2634 is not a char boundary; it is inside 'â' (bytes 2633..2635) of |  Read     AGENTS.md
plan: Reproduce the panic path in redaction and identify the slicing logic at the reported location.
Update redaction to avoid byte-index slicing on UTF-8 strings (use char-safe boundaries or bytes-to-str conversion).
Add or adjust tests to cover non-ASCII inputs and prevent regressions.
Run `cargo test -p ralph` and any targeted checks tied to redaction behavior.
notes: Updated redaction loops to advance on UTF-8 boundaries and avoid byte-to-char casting.
Added a shared helper for safe character copying during redaction scans.
Added a non-ASCII regression test for redact_text.
Ran `cargo test -p ralph`.
tasks.request: This error popped up while running a loop in a different repo. Fix. Error: Provider returned error thread 'main' (15281963) panicked at crates/ralph/src/redaction.rs:235:16: byte index 2634 is not a char boundary; it is inside 'â' (bytes 2633..2635) of |  Read     AGENTS.md

tasks.title: Add two-pass plan mode support for OpenCode runner
evidence: Claude runner supports two_pass_plan but OpenCode does not; documentation says "will expand to OpenCode in the future"
The two_pass_plan_enabled_for: false in task_cmd.rs and scan_cmd.rs comments indicate this is tracked technical debt
plan: Implement run_opencode_two_pass function similar to run_claude_two_pass
Add plan generation phase using RepoPrompt or equivalent tooling
Add implementation phase with auto-approval
Update task_cmd.rs and scan_cmd.rs to enable two_pass_plan for OpenCode
Add tests for OpenCode two-pass behavior

tasks.title: Add queue 'status' support for 'rejected' to allow the user to reject task queue items.
evidence: Rejected is not currently supported as a task status
plan: Add rejected field as a valid option
Wire rejected up the same way the other status values are handled
If a task is in the queue.yaml file running `ralph queue validate` and or `ralph queue repair` should move the task to the bottom of the done.yaml file

tasks.title: Implement task dependency tracking and ordering
evidence: Queue is ordered by priority but there is no dependency system
Tasks cannot specify "must complete after RQ-0100" requirements
Complex multi-step workflows require manual ordering
plan: Add depends_on field to Task contract (list of task IDs)
Validate dependencies exist in queue or done during queue validation
Skip dependent tasks if dependency fails during run loop
Add queue list --filter-deps=RQ-0100 to show dependency chain

tasks.title: Migrate from YAML to JSON for queue and config files
evidence: Current YAML parsing requires ~350 lines of repair code in queue.rs (repair_yaml_structure, repair_yaml_list_fields, repair_yaml_scalars, repair_queue_schema, quote_scalar_for_yaml)
YAML scalar quoting is ambiguous: colons, special characters, and indentation cause parse failures
TUI is planned (RQ-0120) - JSON provides more robust programmatic manipulation for interactive editing
JSON Schema is a standard for defining clear, validated contracts
serde_json is already a dependency (used in runner.rs for stream-json parsing)
User explicitly requested assessment and migration to structured JSON schema
plan: Create JSON Schema definitions for QueueFile, Task, Config, and related types
Replace serde_yaml with serde_json for queue/done/config serialization
Remove all YAML repair logic (~350 lines in queue.rs)
Create migration command (ralph queue migrate-to-json) to convert existing YAML files
Update all documentation references from .yaml to .json
Add backward-compatible read support for YAML during transition period
Update tests to use JSON instead of YAML
Run make ci to verify all tests pass
tasks.request: Assess whether the current YAML approach for task management is really the best approach. User believes moving to a structured JSON schema is a better approach to avoid the parsing complexities that have been detected during the development of this app. Since a TUI is planned for the future, using JSON, which is still human readable, is likely more robust to enable better TUI interaction and reliable task building, tracking, and execution. Having a defined schema makes it clear what fields are supported and what format is expected. If the assessment deems it is warranted, make a plan to implement and then implement it, replacing all existing YAML functionality. Existing queue docs will needs a migration path.

tasks.request: Now that we have switched to JSON for the queue format we need to remove all YAML code from the codebase. Users will be required to migrate or rebuild existing queue docs to the JSON format manually or via external means. This decision is final. Make sure we have a defined JSON schema doc that is our queue JSON schema source of truth and the code utilizes it. That way modifying the schema going forward is much easier.

tasks.title: Refine TUI behavior to eliminate friction and maximize user experience
evidence: User request: "Refine the TUI behavior keeping user experience in mind. Maximize the TUI user experience to eliminate all friction and confusing patterns."
User request: "When users start a task they need to be able to view the output similar to running the loop, or one off execution, in non-interactive mode."
User request: "Non-interactive mode is the preferred user view still but the TUI must be robust and maximize the user experience for users that prefer it."
Current TUI implementation (crates/ralph/src/tui.rs) suspends TUI, runs task, restores - no output visibility during execution
Non-interactive mode shows full runner output (stdout/stderr) in real-time via inherited stdio
TUI lacks live task execution view, progress indication, and output streaming
Task execution in TUI mode (main.rs:454-465) exits TUI, runs task blindly, no status feedback
plan: Add TUI task execution mode: suspend TUI, show live output in scrollable view, restore after completion
Implement split-panel execution view with task output streaming and status updates
Add progress indicators for task execution (runner status, CI gate, git operations)
Add configurable output verbosity levels in TUI (minimal, normal, verbose)
Improve TUI responsiveness during long-running operations (non-blocking redraws)
Add task execution history and result summary in TUI after task completes
Implement graceful TUI recovery from terminal resize and signal handling
Add TUI-specific help mode and key binding reference
Preserve non-interactive mode as default (no behavior changes to --run one/loop without -i)
Add tests for TUI execution view, output streaming, and terminal state management
Ensure CI gate (make ci) output is visible in TUI execution mode
notes: Added OutputHandler callback type to runner.rs for streaming output
Added stream_command() function to support live output capture via callback
Updated all runner functions (run_codex, run_opencode, run_gemini, run_claude) to accept and pass through output_handler
Added output_handler field to RunnerInvocation struct in runutil.rs
Updated run_one_with_id() to accept output_handler parameter
Added Executing mode to AppMode enum with task_id field
Added logs, log_scroll, and autoscroll fields to App struct
Rewrote run_tui() to use polling event loop with mpsc channel for runner events
Added draw_execution_view() function for full-screen output display during task execution
Updated main.rs to create runner factory closure for TUI execution
TUI now executes tasks inline without exiting, showing live output in Executing mode
Non-interactive mode unchanged (output_handler=None defaults to original behavior)
All tests pass (make ci)
tasks.request: Refine the TUI behavior keeping user experience in mind. Maximize the TUI user experience to eliminate all friction and confusing patterns. When users start a task they need to be able to view the output similar to running the loop, or one off execution, in non-interactive mode. Non-interactive mode is the preferred user view still but the TUI must be robust and maximize the user experience for users that prefer it.
